LAW OFFICES 
FROST. KRUP AND ATLAS 
CHARLES H. FROST 
LEONARD G. KRUP 
J. MARK ATLAS 
PROFESSIONAL BUILDING 
134 WEST SYCAMORE STREET 
WILLOWS, CALIFORNIA 95988 
March 11, 1981 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Attention: 
Mrs. Alice Hughes 
TELEPHONE (916) 934-5416 
Re: 
Princeton-Codora-Glenn Irrigation 
District and Other Water Districts 
Dear Mrs. Hughes: 
You will recall that I sent you a letter on 
February 17, 1981 regarding my personal position with 
respect to the Statement of Economic Interests. 
In 
that letter I posed a number. of questions regarding my 
own filing, but to date have not received a response. 
I would appreciate the answers to those ques-
tions I posed, in time to meet the filing deadline. 
Otherwise, please advise whether, if I must ultimately 
file a statement, I may have an extension of time for 
that filing. 
Thank you. 
JMA:eb 


LAW OFFICES 
FR.OST, KR.U P AND ATLAS 
CHARLES H. 
LEONAR.D C. 
J. MARK ATLAS 
PROFESSIONAL BUILDING 
134 WEST SYCAMORE STREET 
WILLOWS, CALIFORNIA 95988 
February 17, 1981 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Attention: 
Mrs. Alice Hughes 
TELEPHONE (916) 934-5416 
Re: 
Princeton-Codora-Glenn Irrigation District 
and Other Water Districts 
Dear Mrs. Hughes: 
In reviewing some recent correspondence from your office 
regarding a change in the Conflict of Interest Code for 
Princeton-Codora-Glenn Irrigation District, a question has 
arisen in my mind regarding whether a filing is necessary 
on my own part. 
In April of 1977, I became an associate of this firm 
and commenced assisting Mr. Charles Frost with his work as 
retained counsel for Princeton-Codora-Glenn Irrigation District, 
Kanawha Water District, and Orland-Artois Water District. 
Parenthetically, you should note that the latter two districts' 
code reviewing authority is the Board of Supervisors of Glenn 
County. 
This office also performed work from time to time for 
Colusa County Water District, although not at that time as 
retained counsel. 
On December 29, 1978, Mr. Krup and I became partners 
in this office, and Mr. Frost withdrew as a partner to become 
"of counsel". 
Finally, in November of 1980, our office was 
placed on a regular retainer by Colusa County Water District. 
In the last 18 months or so, I have been increasingly 
assuming from Mr. Frost the duties of primary counsel to these 
districts. 
Annually, during the entire period noted above, Mr. Frost 
has filed the Statement of Economic Interests with each of 
these districts, but I have never done so. 
Each of the Codes 
of the districts includes as a "Designated Employee" the 


, . 
,Fair Political Practices Commission 
Page Two 
February 17, 1981 
retained attorney as one who must file such Statements, 
but none of those Codes defines the term "retained attorney". 
I am concerned, however, that such a filing by me m;ight be 
necessary in addition to the filing made by Mr. Frost; and 
I am, of course, willing to file the necessary statements. 
Before doing so, however, I wished to address this corres-
pondence to you to determine (1) whether I must file such a 
Statement, and (2) the type of Statement (Uinitial lt
, "assuming 
office" or "annual") and the period covered. 
I appreciate your guidance in this matter and will 
await your response. 
Sincerely y~Sf 
J. 
JMA/cd

sections.question: whether a filing is necessary on my own part; (1) whether I must file such a Statement, and (2) the type of Statement ("initial", "assuming office" or "annual") and the period covered.
sections.conclusion: the primary retained attorney for the agencies, according to the information in your letters, appears to need to file a statement under the Code. The filing should be an assuming office statement if this occurred when you assumed primary responsibility for the agencies. A reasonable understanding would be to consider January 1, 1981 as the date you took over as the primary retained attorney for the agencies.
sections.question_synthetic: Does J. Mark Atlas, as an associate and increasingly primary counsel to several water irrigation districts, need to file a Statement of Economic Interests, and if so, what type of statement and covering what period?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that as primary retained counsel for the water districts, Mr. Atlas should file an 'assuming office' Statement of Economic Interests, with January 1, 1981 as the effective date, and then file annual statements by March 30 of each subsequent year.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains significant OCR scanning artifacts and damage that obscure portions of the FPPC response letter. The key question and conclusion sections were reconstructed from the incoming request letters and the partial FPPC response visible in the document. The document bundle includes two incoming request letters (Feb 17 and Mar 11, 1981) followed by the FPPC's March 30, 1981 response letter with visible damage/scanning issues.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: whether a filing is necessary on my own part; (1) whether I must file such a Statement, and (2) the type of Statement ("initial", "assuming office" or "annual") and the period covered.

CONCLUSION: the primary retained attorney for the agencies, according to the information in your letters, appears to need to file a statement under the Code. The filing should be an assuming office statement if this occurred when you assumed primary responsibility for the agencies. A reasonable understanding would be to consider January 1, 1981 as the date you took over as the primary retained attorney for the agencies.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to J. Mark Atlas's inquiry about whether he must file Statements of Economic Interests for water districts where he is increasingly serving as primary counsel, advising that he should file an 'assuming office' statement dated January 1, 1981, followed by annual filings.
